draft.introduction: Luke 24:44–53 records Jesus’ final instructions after the resurrection, emphasizing that the scriptures must be fulfilled. It serves as the bridge between the Gospel of Luke and the book of Acts.

translation.text: Then he said to them, ‘These are my words that I spoke to you while I was still with you, that everything written about me in the Law of Moses and the Prophets and the Psalms must be fulfilled.’ Then he opened their minds to understand the Scriptures.
translation.original_text: εἶπεν δὲ πρὸς αὐτούς · οὗτοι οἱ λόγοι μου οὓς ἐλάλησα πρὸς ὑμᾶς ἔτι ὢν σὺν ὑμῖν

translator_notes.note: The threefold division—Law, Prophets, and Psalms—reflects the emerging tripartite structure of the Hebrew Bible canon. Luke’s Jesus is the only Gospel figure to explicitly reference this three-part division.
